ApproveOrRejectReimbursementRequestBody.description:
Approve or reject an employee reimbursement request for an out-of-pocket expense.

Use this tool when the user wants to approve or reject a reimbursement.
Reimbursements are employee-submitted requests for reimbursement of out-of-pocket expenses.
Common usage patterns: "approve this reimbursement", "reject the expense", "yes, reimburse them", "no, don't reimburse this".
This tool is specifically for employee reimbursements and is NOT for approving/rejecting card transactions
or unified requests (spend allocations, purchase orders).

This will be recorded in the approval history and may be visible to other approvers and the reimbursement submitter. Use this when the user provides context like 'approve it because...' or 'reject because...'.

ApproveOrRejectRequestRequestBody.description:
Approve or reject a pending unified request for spend allocation (fund), purchase order, or procurement approval.

Use this tool when a user wants to approve or reject a specific request they have identified or referenced.
Common usage patterns: "approve this request", "reject this request", "accept the request", "deny this",
"authorize this", "yes approve it", "decline this request".
This tool is specifically for unified requests (spend allocations, purchase orders, procurement approvals)
and is NOT for approving expenses like corporate card transactions or reimbursements.
The user must have proper approval authority for the request.

ApproveOrRejectTransactionRequestBody.description:
Approve or reject a transaction awaiting your review.

Use this tool when the user wants to approve a transaction, send it back for
revisions, or require the cardholder to repay the full amount. The acting user
must have the proper approval authority for the transaction. If the user provides
a written justification (e.g., “reject this because the receipt is missing”), pass
that verbatim via `user_reason`—do not invent your own rationale.

AskHelpCenterRequestBody.description:
Ask a question and get an answer from Ramp's help center.

This tool uses an LLM to read through relevant help center articles and extract
the most pertinent information to answer your question. It provides synthesized answers
combining multiple sources with extracted relevant snippets and source attribution.

IMPORTANT: If you have other tools that can actually perform the action a user is asking about,
you should use those tools instead of or in addition to providing help center information.
For example, if a user says "how can I upload a receipt", you should tell them they can
upload a receipt in the chat directly, even if you also find a help center article that
explains how to upload receipts in the web app.

Use this tool when you need to:
- Ask specific questions about Ramp products and features
- Get answers that combine information from multiple articles
- Understand complex workflows
- Get direct answers rather than searching through full articles

EditReimbursementRequestBody.description:
Edit a reimbursement field. The only supported edit types are memo, funds, and accounting category.
This tool allows updating specific fields of a reimbursement that the user has
permission to edit. Provide at least one field to update.

After successfully editing the reimbursement:
1. Check missing_items to see what is required
2. For items that CAN be filled via the agent (memo, accounting categories, funds):
   - Request the missing item from the user
   - Include the relevant suggestions (suggested_memos, suggested_codings, or suggested_funds)
3. For items that CANNOT be filled via the agent (trip, attendee, mileage_locations, or if all agent-fillable items are complete):
   - ALWAYS provide the reimbursement_link URL as a clickable link in your response
   - Format it as markdown: [link text](reimbursement_link)
   - Example: "You can complete your reimbursement here: [Complete reimbursement](https://app.ramp.com/s/reimbursements/xxx)"

Items are requested in this order: receipt, memo, accounting categories, funds.

If there are no missing items left, the agent should confirm with the user if they want to submit the reimbursement.

memo.description: The memo/description text to set for the reimbursement

For BOOLEAN and SINGLE_CHOICE tracking categories, option_selection should be the UUID of the tracking category option. For FREE_FORM_TEXT tracking categories, option_selection should be the freeform text value.

EditTransactionRequestBody.description:
Edit a transaction field. The supported edit types are memo, funds, accounting category, trip, and attendees.
This tool allows updating specific fields of a transaction that the user has
permission to edit. Provide at least one field to update.

After successfully editing the transaction:
1. Check missing_items to see what is required
2. For items that CAN be filled via the agent (memo, accounting categories, funds, trip, attendees):
   - Request the missing item from the user
   - Include the relevant suggestions (suggested_memos, suggested_codings, or suggested_funds)
3. For items that CANNOT be filled via the agent (if all agent-fillable items are complete):
   - ALWAYS provide the transaction_link URL as a clickable link in your response
   - Format it as markdown: [link text](transaction_link)
   - Example: "You can complete your transaction here: [Complete transaction](https://app.ramp.com/transactions/xxx)"

Items are requested in this order: receipt, memo, accounting categories, funds, trip, attendees.

If there are no missing items left, the agent should confirm with the user if they want to submit the transaction.

attendee_selections.description: Attendee selections: attendee_uuids (Ramp users), non_ramp_attendees (external people), include_self_as_attendee (auto-add self). This is an upsert - provide the complete set.

fund_uuid.description: UUID of the fund (spend allocation) to charge this transaction to.
fund_uuid.title: Fund Uuid

memo.description: The memo/description text to set for the transaction. Set to empty string ('') if clearing the memo.
memo.title: Memo

tracking_category_selections.description: Tracking/accounting category assignments (e.g., category, department, project). Must provide both category and option_selection. For BOOLEAN and SINGLE_CHOICE tracking categories, option_selection should be the UUID of the tracking category option, or None to clear the field (only when user explicitly replies 'none' and the business allows it). For FREE_FORM_TEXT tracking categories, option_selection should be the freeform text value. Retrieve these from codings you suggest to the user. Only the codings that are to be updated need to be provided.
tracking_category_selections.title: Tracking Category Selections

transaction_uuid.description: UUID of the transaction to modify. Call GetUserTransactions to determine the UUID if it cannot be obtained from context.
transaction_uuid.title: Transaction Uuid

trip_selection.description: Trip selection configuration. Provide a trip_uuid to assign/change the transaction to a trip (or None to remove), or set mark_not_part_of_trip to True to mark as not part of any trip.

user_submitted_fields.description: Fields where the user DIRECTLY provided the specific value in their message. Only include a field if the user explicitly stated that exact value. Do NOT include fields where you (the agent) decided/inferred the value based on context. Examples - INCLUDE in user_submitted_fields: User says 'Team lunch' -> include memo. User says 'Use the Travel fund' -> include fund_uuid. Examples - DO NOT INCLUDE: You decide to change fund based on memo content -> do not include fund_uuid. You infer tracking category from transaction details -> do not include tracking_category_selections.
user_submitted_fields.title: User Submitted Fields

FilterCriteriaRequestBody.description: A single filter to apply to transaction search.

filter.description: The specific value to filter by. Must match exactly for categories (e.g., 'Taxi and Rideshare', 'Airlines'). Can be partial match for merchant, spend_allocation, trip, and tracking_category.
filter.title: Filter

filter_type.description:
Type of filter to apply:
- 'category': Expense category. Valid categories: Airlines, Car Rental, Lodging, Taxi and Rideshare, Fuel and Gas, Restaurants, Alcohol and Bars, Office supplies and cleaning, Office, Electronics, Clothing, Books and Newspapers, General Merchandise, Streaming Services, Entertainment, Supermarkets and Grocery Stores, Medical, Pet, Shipping, Utilities, Professional Services, Advertising, Fees and Financial institutions, Taxes and tax preparation, Other
- 'merchant': Specific merchant/vendor name like 'Starbucks', 'Amazon', 'Uber', 'Delta Airlines'
- 'spend_allocation': Spending allocation/budget/fund name like 'Marketing Budget', 'Engineering Fund', 'Wellness Stipend'
- 'trip': Business trip/event/conference name. IMPORTANT: Trip names are the EVENT names (e.g., 'ERP Conference', 'Q4 Sales Offsite', 'Client Summit 2024'), NOT city/location names. When a user asks about 'travel to/from [city] for [event]', the trip filter should be the EVENT name (e.g., 'ERP Conference'), not the destination city (e.g., 'New York City'). Cities mentioned are travel origins/destinations, not trip names.
- 'tracking_category': Accounting coding / tracking category option name or code. Use this to filter transactions by their accounting coding values (e.g., 'Engineering', 'Marketing', 'US East', 'ENG-001'). Searches across all active tracking categories (Department, Cost Center, GL Account, etc.). Only available for businesses with a connected accounting integration.
filter_type.title: Filter Type

GetBookingsRequestBody.description:
Retrieves flight and hotel bookings for the user.

Use this tool when users ask about their:
- Hotel bookings or reservations
- Flight bookings
- Travel bookings in general
- Status of a recent booking request

Returns both confirmed bookings and pending/failed booking requests in unified lists.
Check the status field: PENDING_APPROVAL, PROCESSING, or FAILED indicate unconfirmed requests.
If status is FAILED, the error_message field explains why.

IMPORTANT: Cancelled and failed bookings do NOT block new bookings. If a user has a prior
booking with status CANCELLED or a failed request with status FAILED, they can still make
a new booking for the same route, hotel, or trip. Always allow the user to proceed with a
new search and booking when they ask to rebook after a cancellation or failure.

IMPORTANT: When presenting results, use Rich Object References (ROR) format:
- For hotels: [Hotel Name](hotel-uuid) e.g., [Marriott Downtown](abc123-def456)
- For flights: [Flight Info](flight-uuid) e.g., [UA 100 SFO → JFK](abc123-def456)
The 'id' field in each booking is the UUID to use in the reference.

GetTrackingCategoriesRequestBody.description:
Retrieve all active tracking categories for the current business.

Tracking categories are used to organize and categorize transactions in the accounting system.
Common examples include:
- Departments (e.g., Engineering, Sales, Marketing)
- Classes or Business Units
- Locations (e.g., US East, US West)
- Projects
- Customers
- Cost Centers

This tool returns only the tracking category metadata, not the available options.
To get the options for a specific tracking category, use GetTrackingCategoryOptions.

This tool should mainly be used as a helper tool to identify tracking categories when users want to set
accounting categories on transactions.

include_hidden.description: Set to True to include hidden categories. By default, only visible categories are returned.
include_hidden.title: Include Hidden

transaction_uuid.description: UUID of the transaction being coded. When provided, ensures the correct business context is used to look up tracking categories.
transaction_uuid.title: Transaction Uuid

GetTrackingCategoriesRequestBody.title: GetTrackingCategories

GetTrackingCategoriesResult.description: Response containing all active tracking categories.
GetTrackingCategoriesResult.title: GetTrackingCategoriesResult

GetTrackingCategoryOptionsRequestBody.description:
Retrieve options for a specific tracking category with optional search.

Use this tool to get the available options (e.g., specific departments, locations, projects)
for a tracking category. This tool supports pagination for tracking categories that have
large numbers of options (some businesses can have millions of options). It is highly encouraged
to use the query_string parameter to filter options by name when possible.

When being used to set a coding for a transaction, you MUST pass the transaction_uuid parameter and set include_hidden to False
to filter out hidden options based on subsidiary/conditional filtering. The transaction_uuid should only not be set when
doing generic searches over tracking category options.

Search behavior:
- When query_string is provided and semantic search is enabled (via feature flag), the tool uses
  turbopuffer semantic search combined with ILIKE for better results. Pagination is not supported
  in this mode and falls back to the paginated endpoint on failure.
- Otherwise, the tool uses simple ILIKE filtering with pagination support.

Workflow:
1. First call GetTrackingCategories to get the list of tracking categories
2. Then call this tool with the tracking_category_uuid to get the options, using query_string when possible and the transaction_uuid to be coded
3. If next_page_cursor is returned, call again with that cursor to get more results if necessary

This tool should mainly be used to identify tracking category options when users want to set
accounting categories on transactions. There is no need to use this tool if the user wants to use a
suggested coding that we already know the option UUID for.

include_hidden.description: Set to True to include hidden options. By default, only visible options are returned.
include_hidden.title: Include Hidden

next_page_cursor.description: Use this to get additional results after the first page. Copy the next_page_cursor value from a previous response to continue pagination
next_page_cursor.title: Next Page Cursor

page_size.description: Maximum number of options to return in one request. Use smaller numbers (10-25) for quick overviews, larger numbers (50-100) for comprehensive views
page_size.title: Page Size

query_string.description: Optional search string to filter options by name. Useful for finding specific options.
query_string.title: Query String

tracking_category_uuid.description: UUID of the tracking category to get options for. Obtain this from GetTrackingCategories.
tracking_category_uuid.title: Tracking Category Uuid

transaction_uuid.description: UUID of the transaction to filter options for when coding it. When provided, applies conditional filtering based on the transaction's user, department, location, etc. to exclude options that are hidden for that specific transaction context.

GetUserFundsRequestBody.description:
Retrieve funds (also known as cards or budgets) that belong to the current user or other users.

Use this tool when users ask about:
- Their funds, cards, or budgets (e.g., "show me my funds", "what cards do I have?")
- What they can buy from a specific fund
- Why their card is locked or not working (e.g., "why is my card locked?", "why can't I use my card?")
- Fund balances, restrictions, or lock status

When querying for a specific person's funds, first call GetOrgChart to get their UUID,
then use the user_uuids parameter. For queries about "my funds", use funds_to_retrieve=MY_FUNDS.
To search by fund name, use search_by_fund_display_name.
For card lock questions, set include_lock_info=True to get lock details.

for_transaction_id.description: When provided, retrieves only the funds that this specific transaction can be reassigned to. When this parameter is set, all other parameters are ignored.
for_transaction_id.title: For Transaction Id

funds_to_retrieve.description: Scope of funds to retrieve. Use MY_FUNDS ONLY when the user explicitly asks about 'my funds'. Use ALL_FUNDS for general searches (e.g., searching by fund name) or when no specific owner is mentioned. NEVER use this parameter when the user mentions a specific person - use user_uuids instead.

include_balance.description: Did the user request something related to the balance of the fund? Is the user trying to check if they used their funds this week/month?
include_balance.title: Include Balance

include_lock_info.description: Set to True when the user asks about locks (e.g., 'why is my card locked?', 'how do I unlock my card?'). Adds lock details for the fund and its members, including information about missing policy items.
include_lock_info.title: Include Lock Info

include_members.description: Set to True when the user asks about who has access to a fund or who are the members of a fund. Returns the names of users who have access to each fund.
include_members.title: Include Members

include_restrictions.description: Set to True when the user asks about fund restrictions such as: merchant/vendor whitelists or blacklists, category whitelists or blacklists, transaction amount limits, auto-lock dates, expiration dates, or interval/reset dates. This adds a lot of context, so only include it if needed.
include_restrictions.title: Include Restrictions

search_by_fund_display_name.description: Text search to filter by the display name of the fund itself (e.g., 'office supplies', 'conference budget', 'equipment fund'). This searches what the fund is called, NOT who owns it. NEVER use this parameter when the user mentions a person's name - person names are not fund display names. If searching by owner/person, use user_uuids instead.
search_by_fund_display_name.title: Search By Fund Display Name

state.description: Filter funds by state. Use ACTIVE to show only active funds, TERMINATED for terminated funds, or ARCHIVED for archived funds. If not provided, returns funds in all states.

user_uuids.description: REQUIRED when the user asks about a specific person's funds. Workflow: (1) First call GetOrgChart to find the person and get their UUID, (2) Then pass that UUID here. Use this for any query mentioning a person by name. Do NOT use funds_to_retrieve when asking about a specific person.
user_uuids.title: User Uuids

GetUserFundsRequestBody.title: GetUserFunds

GetUserTransactionsRequestBody.description:
Search and retrieve expense transactions from Ramp

This tool helps you find card transactions. Use this tool when users want to:
- View their recent transactions or expenses
- Search for specific purchases by merchant, amount, or description
- Find transactions that need approval
- Analyze spending patterns
- Review transactions by specific team members
- Look up transactions with missing documentation and retrieve suggestions
- View declined transactions (set state='declined')

The tool returns structured transaction data including amounts, merchants, dates,
employee names, policy compliance status, and missing documentation requirements + suggestions

IMPORTANT: By default, declined transactions are NOT included in results. If a user asks
about declined, rejected, or failed transactions, you MUST set state='declined' to include them.

If the transaction the user is looking for or discussing is not returned, try again
with a different business_uuid — the user may have accounts across multiple businesses.
You can call this tool multiple times with different business_uuid values to get
transactions from all businesses for a given user.

business_uuid.description: The business UUID to scope the transaction search to. When the user has multiple businesses, use this to specify which business to query. If not available from context, retrieve it from GetSimplifiedUserDetail. If not provided, defaults to the first user's business.
business_uuid.title: Business Uuid

details_to_include_in_response.description: Additional detailed information to include for each transaction. Options: policy assessment data (for reviewers approving transactions), missing documentation details (what receipts or approvals are needed), suggestions for transaction items (memos, codings, and funds), and submitted items (receipts, trips, attendees, and accounting categories currently on the transaction)
details_to_include_in_response.title: Details To Include In Response

filters.description: Advanced filters to narrow down transactions by specific criteria like merchant categories, spending allocations, or business trips. Multiple filters use conjunction logic (AND) - e.g., merchant='Starbucks' AND fund='Marketing Budget'

from_date.description: Start date for filtering transactions (YYYY-MM-DD format). Only transactions on or after this date will be included. Example: '2024-01-01'
from_date.title: From Date

include_count.description: Whether to include the total count of matching transactions in the response. Set to false only if you just need the transaction data and want faster responses
include_count.title: Include Count

next_page_cursor.description: Use this to get additional results after the first page. Copy the next_page_cursor value from a previous response to continue pagination
next_page_cursor.title: Next Page Cursor

page_size.description: Maximum number of transactions to return in one request. Use smaller numbers (10-25) for quick overviews, larger numbers (50-200) for comprehensive analysis
page_size.title: Page Size

reason_memo_merchant_or_user_name_text_s.description: Text search across merchant names, transaction descriptions/memos, and employee names. Must be at least 3 characters long. Examples: 'Starbucks', 'office supplies', 'John Smith'. Trip names, attendees and fund budget names are not searchable from this field
reason_memo_merchant_or_user_name_text_s.title: Reason Memo Merchant Or User Name Text Search

sort.description: How to sort the results. You can sort by user (employee name), merchant name, or transaction date in ascending or descending order. By default we sort by transaction date descending

state.description: Filter transactions by their state. Use 'declined' when the user asks about declined or failed transactions. Use 'pending' for pending/processing transactions, 'cleared' for completed transactions, or 'all' to include all states. When not specified, only non-declined transactions are returned by default.

to_date.description: End date for filtering transactions (YYYY-MM-DD format). Only transactions on or before this date will be included. Example: '2024-12-31'
to_date.title: To Date

transactions_to_retrieve.description: Which set of transactions to search: your own transactions, transactions awaiting your approval, all company transactions (if you have access), or transactions made by your team members

LockOrUnlockCardRequestBody.description:
Lock or unlock a card.

Use this tool when:
- A user wants to temporarily lock their card (lock)
- A user wants to unlock a previously locked card (unlock)

IMPORTANT: Locking a card is a destructive action. Before calling this tool with action='lock', you MUST:
1. Confirm the card details with the user (show them which card will be affected)
2. Explain that locking the card will prevent all transactions until unlocked
3. Ask the user to explicitly confirm they want to lock the card (e.g., "Yes, lock my card")
4. Only proceed if the user has explicitly confirmed the action

Do NOT lock a card based on assumptions about suspicious activity or compromised cards.
The user must explicitly request and confirm the lock action.

For unlocking, confirmation is not required since this is a non-destructive action.

id.description: The UUID of the card to lock or unlock.
id.title: Id

PostCommentRequestBody.description:
Post a comment on a Ramp object (transaction, bill, reimbursement, etc).

If there is any ambiguity as to whether the user's message is meant to be
posted to the thread, ask them for confirmation first: 'Do you want me to post that
as a comment reply on your [XYZ] transaction/reimbursement/bill/etc?'.

How can you determine if the user's message is meant to be a reply to a comment?
  - If the only other messages in the conversation history are notifications about comments or mentions in Ramp, that is a very strong signal that the user's most recent message is meant to be a reply to the comment thread. In this case, you may use this tool immediately to post the comment without confirmation.
  - If a recent assistant message is a notification about a comment or mention, that is a strong signal that the user's most recent message is meant to be a reply to that comment. Even if their reply seems irrelevant or unrelated to Ramp, you may ask "Would you like me to post that as a comment on your...?" - prefer asking the user instead of refusing to serve the request at all.

mention_user_uuids.description: List of user UUIDs to @-mention in the comment. If the user is replying to a recent comment or mention notification from the assistant, you must use this to set the UUID of the user they are replying to. The mentioned users will be notified and their names will be prepended to the comment message as @-mentions. Extract user UUIDs from the context when the user wants to mention someone (e.g., 'tell Bob that...' -> find Bob's UUID and include it here). If the user's message includes Slack-formatted @-mentions, use the ResolveSlackMention tool to turn those into user UUIDs
mention_user_uuids.title: Mention User Uuids

message.description: The comment message text. If the user sends 'its done' -> post 'its done' to the comment thread. If the user sends 'its done' -> DO NOT post 'The receipt has been uploaded.' or any other variation. Do not correct spelling or punctuation or grammar. Do not include user @-mentions in this field - use mention_user_uuids instead and they will be added automatically. You may also replace Slack-formatted @-mentions with a user's full name by resolving it with the ResolveSlackMention tool.
message.title: Message

Used by AI agents (Omni Chat, Ramp Assist) to understand what actions are available

SearchTagCategoryRequestBody.description:
This enum represents all possible categories of filters that can be applied to a
table search across all of Ramp.

Subsets of these categories are used in different surfaces, such as transactions,
reimbursements, bills, and reporting. The specific subsets used by each surface
is declared below.

The values of these enums are persisted in the database as a part of saved table
views and reports, and thus they should never be renamed or deleted without migrating
all saved table views and reports first, or ensuring that said enums are not
currently being persisted in the database.

Similarly, each surface should not remove any supported categories without ensuring
that the categories are not currently being used in any saved table views or reports
for that surface.

post.description:
Activates an unactivated card for the user. If the last four digits do not match
any of the user's cards, returns available card last-four values so the correct
digits can be provided.
post.summary: Activate a card by its last four digits

post.description:
Use this tool when the user asks about expense policy rules, spending limits,
fund restrictions, or whether certain purchases are allowed under company policy.
post.summary: Get an answer to a question about the expense policy or funds (amount, restrictions, etc)

post.description:
Use this tool when the user wants to approve or reject a bill that is pending their approval.
You can find bills for approval by the current user by using the GetBillsForApproval tool.
Before using this tool, ask the user if they would like to include a reason or note
for their decision. Reason is optional, so you may call this tool with no reason
if the user declines to provide one.
post.summary: Approve or reject a bill that requires approval

post.description:
Use this tool when the user wants to approve or reject a reimbursement.
Reimbursements are employee-submitted requests for reimbursement of out-of-pocket expenses.
Common usage patterns: "approve this reimbursement", "reject the expense", "yes, reimburse them", "no, don't reimburse this".
This tool is specifically for employee reimbursements and is NOT for approving/rejecting card transactions
or unified requests (spend allocations, purchase orders).
post.summary: Approve or reject an employee reimbursement request for an out-of-pocket expense

post.description:
Use this tool when a user wants to approve or reject a specific request they have identified or referenced.
Common usage patterns: "approve this request", "reject this request", "accept the request", "deny this",
"authorize this", "yes approve it", "decline this request".
This tool is specifically for unified requests (spend allocations, purchase orders, procurement approvals)
and is NOT for approving expenses like corporate card transactions or reimbursements.
The user must have proper approval authority for the request.
post.summary: Approve or reject a pending unified request for spend allocation (fund), purchase order, or procurement approval

post.description:
Use this tool when the user wants to approve a transaction, send it back for
revisions, or require the cardholder to repay the full amount. The acting user
must have the proper approval authority for the transaction. If the user provides
a written justification (e.g., “reject this because the receipt is missing”), pass
that verbatim via `user_reason`—do not invent your own rationale.
post.summary: Approve or reject a transaction awaiting your review

post.description:
This tool uses an LLM to read through relevant help center articles and extract
the most pertinent information to answer your question. It provides synthesized answers
combining multiple sources with extracted relevant snippets and source attribution.

IMPORTANT: If you have other tools that can actually perform the action a user is asking about,
you should use those tools instead of or in addition to providing help center information.
For example, if a user says "how can I upload a receipt", you should tell them they can
upload a receipt in the chat directly, even if you also find a help center article that
explains how to upload receipts in the web app.

Use this tool when you need to:
- Ask specific questions about Ramp products and features
- Get answers that combine information from multiple articles
- Understand complex workflows
- Get direct answers rather than searching through full articles
post.summary: Ask a question and get an answer from Ramp's help center

post.description:
Use this tool when the user wants to link an already-created receipt to a specific transaction.
The receipt must have been previously created (e.g. via CreateReceipt).
post.summary: Attach an existing receipt to a transaction
Create a new trip for the acting user

post.description:
The only supported edit types are memo, funds, and accounting category.
This tool allows updating specific fields of a reimbursement that the user has
permission to edit. Provide at least one field to update.

After successfully editing the reimbursement:
1. Check missing_items to see what is required
2. For items that CAN be filled via the agent (memo, accounting categories, funds):
   - Request the missing item from the user
   - Include the relevant suggestions (suggested_memos, suggested_codings, or suggested_funds)
3. For items that CANNOT be filled via the agent (trip, attendee, mileage_locations, or if all agent-fillable items are complete):
   - ALWAYS provide the reimbursement_link URL as a clickable link in your response
   - Format it as markdown: [link text](reimbursement_link)
   - Example: "You can complete your reimbursement here: [Complete reimbursement](https://app.ramp.com/s/reimbursements/xxx)"

Items are requested in this order: receipt, memo, accounting categories, funds.

If there are no missing items left, the agent should confirm with the user if they want to submit the reimbursement.
post.summary: Edit a reimbursement field

post.description:
The supported edit types are memo, funds, accounting category, trip, and attendees.
This tool allows updating specific fields of a transaction that the user has
permission to edit. Provide at least one field to update.

After successfully editing the transaction:
1. Check missing_items to see what is required
2. For items that CAN be filled via the agent (memo, accounting categories, funds, trip, attendees):
   - Request the missing item from the user
   - Include the relevant suggestions (suggested_memos, suggested_codings, or suggested_funds)
3. For items that CANNOT be filled via the agent (if all agent-fillable items are complete):
   - ALWAYS provide the transaction_link URL as a clickable link in your response
   - Format it as markdown: [link text](transaction_link)
   - Example: "You can complete your transaction here: [Complete transaction](https://app.ramp.com/transactions/xxx)"

Items are requested in this order: receipt, memo, accounting categories, funds, trip, attendees.

If there are no missing items left, the agent should confirm with the user if they want to submit the transaction.
post.summary: Edit a transaction field

not yet been submitted, use GetDraftBillDetails instead.

Use this tool when the user wants to see bills they need to approve or asks about
pending approvals. This returns only bills where the user is the next approver in

post.description:
Use this tool when users ask about their:
- Hotel bookings or reservations
- Flight bookings
- Travel bookings in general
- Status of a recent booking request

Returns both confirmed bookings and pending/failed booking requests in unified lists.
Check the status field: PENDING_APPROVAL, PROCESSING, or FAILED indicate unconfirmed requests.
If status is FAILED, the error_message field explains why.

IMPORTANT: Cancelled and failed bookings do NOT block new bookings. If a user has a prior
booking with status CANCELLED or a failed request with status FAILED, they can still make
a new booking for the same route, hotel, or trip. Always allow the user to proceed with a
new search and booking when they ask to rebook after a cancellation or failure.

IMPORTANT: When presenting results, use Rich Object References (ROR) format:
- For hotels: [Hotel Name](hotel-uuid) e.g., [Marriott Downtown](abc123-def456)
- For flights: [Flight Info](flight-uuid) e.g., [UA 100 SFO → JFK](abc123-def456)
The 'id' field in each booking is the UUID to use in the reference.
post.summary: Retrieves flight and hotel bookings for the user

post.description: Returns ongoing, upcoming, and recently completed trips for the acting user.
post.summary: Fetch trips that could be relevant to the current transaction

post.description:
IMPORTANT: Only call this tool on transactions that are already known to be
declined. If you need to find declined transactions first, use GetUserTransactions
with state='declined'. Do NOT call this on approved or pending transactions —
it will return a not_declined error.

This tool provides:
- Human-readable explanation of the decline reason
- Structured remediation actions the user or admin can take
- The SMS/push notification message sent to the user
- Transaction details (amount, merchant, card, timestamp)

Use this when a user asks why their transaction was declined or what they can do about it.
post.summary: Get a detailed explanation of why a transaction was declined

post.description:
Use this tool when the user is viewing a draft bill page (e.g. /bill-pay/bills/list/edit-draft/{uuid})
or asks about a bill that hasn't been submitted yet. Draft bills are also known as provisional bills.
Returns vendor info, amount, line items, memo, and other metadata for the draft.
post.summary: Get details about a draft (provisional) bill that has not yet been submitted for approval

post.description:
Returns locations where flights can be booked from or to, which may include:

- City groups with multiple airports (e.g., NYC includes JFK, LGA, EWR)
- Major travel destinations with flight availability
post.summary: Resolves locations (airports/cities) available for booking a flight from user intent

post.description:
Use this tool when users ask about:
- Their funds, cards, or budgets (e.g., "show me my funds", "what cards do I have?")
- What they can buy from a specific fund
- Why their card is locked or not working (e.g., "why is my card locked?", "why can't I use my card?")
- Fund balances, restrictions, or lock status

When querying for a specific person's funds, first call GetOrgChart to get their UUID,
then use the user_uuids parameter. For queries about "my funds", use funds_to_retrieve=MY_FUNDS.
To search by fund name, use search_by_fund_display_name.
For card lock questions, set include_lock_info=True to get lock details.
post.summary: Retrieve funds (also known as cards or budgets) that belong to the current user or other users

post.description:
Tracking categories are used to organize and categorize transactions in the accounting system.
Common examples include:
- Departments (e.g., Engineering, Sales, Marketing)
- Classes or Business Units
- Locations (e.g., US East, US West)
- Projects
- Customers
- Cost Centers

This tool returns only the tracking category metadata, not the available options.
To get the options for a specific tracking category, use GetTrackingCategoryOptions.

This tool should mainly be used as a helper tool to identify tracking categories when users want to set
accounting categories on transactions.
post.summary: Retrieve all active tracking categories for the current business

post.description:
Use this tool to get the available options (e.g., specific departments, locations, projects)
for a tracking category. This tool supports pagination for tracking categories that have
large numbers of options (some businesses can have millions of options). It is highly encouraged
to use the query_string parameter to filter options by name when possible.

When being used to set a coding for a transaction, you MUST pass the transaction_uuid parameter and set include_hidden to False
to filter out hidden options based on subsidiary/conditional filtering. The transaction_uuid should only not be set when
doing generic searches over tracking category options.

Search behavior:
- When query_string is provided and semantic search is enabled (via feature flag), the tool uses
  turbopuffer semantic search combined with ILIKE for better results. Pagination is not supported
  in this mode and falls back to the paginated endpoint on failure.
- Otherwise, the tool uses simple ILIKE filtering with pagination support.

Workflow:
1. First call GetTrackingCategories to get the list of tracking categories
2. Then call this tool with the tracking_category_uuid to get the options, using query_string when possible and the transaction_uuid to be coded
3. If next_page_cursor is returned, call again with that cursor to get more results if necessary

This tool should mainly be used to identify tracking category options when users want to set
accounting categories on transactions. There is no need to use this tool if the user wants to use a
suggested coding that we already know the option UUID for.
post.summary: Retrieve options for a specific tracking category with optional search
Check what receipts, memos, or accounting items are missing from a specific transaction
Get AI-suggested memo/note options for a specific transaction

post.description:
This tool helps you find card transactions. Use this tool when users want to:
- View their recent transactions or expenses
- Search for specific purchases by merchant, amount, or description
- Find transactions that need approval
- Analyze spending patterns
- Review transactions by specific team members
- Look up transactions with missing documentation and retrieve suggestions
- View declined transactions (set state='declined')

The tool returns structured transaction data including amounts, merchants, dates,
employee names, policy compliance status, and missing documentation requirements + suggestions

IMPORTANT: By default, declined transactions are NOT included in results. If a user asks
about declined, rejected, or failed transactions, you MUST set state='declined' to include them.

If the transaction the user is looking for or discussing is not returned, try again
with a different business_uuid — the user may have accounts across multiple businesses.
You can call this tool multiple times with different business_uuid values to get
transactions from all businesses for a given user.
post.summary: Search and retrieve expense transactions from Ramp
Fetch trips for the acting user with optional filters
List active cards for the current user

post.description:
Use this tool when:
- A user wants to temporarily lock their card (lock)
- A user wants to unlock a previously locked card (unlock)

IMPORTANT: Locking a card is a destructive action. Before calling this tool with action='lock', you MUST:
1. Confirm the card details with the user (show them which card will be affected)
2. Explain that locking the card will prevent all transactions until unlocked
3. Ask the user to explicitly confirm they want to lock the card (e.g., "Yes, lock my card")
4. Only proceed if the user has explicitly confirmed the action

Do NOT lock a card based on assumptions about suspicious activity or compromised cards.
The user must explicitly request and confirm the lock action.

For unlocking, confirmation is not required since this is a non-destructive action.
post.summary: Lock or unlock a card

post.description: Should be called whenever the user says "OOPS" or if the user says something along the lines of "I did not receive a receipt ...". If the user already flagged the transaction as having no receipt, then you may need to call the ProvideNoReceiptReason tool.
post.summary: Flag a transaction as having no receipt

post.description:
If there is any ambiguity as to whether the user's message is meant to be
posted to the thread, ask them for confirmation first: 'Do you want me to post that
as a comment reply on your [XYZ] transaction/reimbursement/bill/etc?'.

How can you determine if the user's message is meant to be a reply to a comment?
  - If the only other messages in the conversation history are notifications about comments or mentions in Ramp, that is a very strong signal that the user's most recent message is meant to be a reply to the comment thread. In this case, you may use this tool immediately to post the comment without confirmation.
  - If a recent assistant message is a notification about a comment or mention, that is a strong signal that the user's most recent message is meant to be a reply to that comment. Even if their reply seems irrelevant or unrelated to Ramp, you may ask "Would you like me to post that as a comment on your...?" - prefer asking the user instead of refusing to serve the request at all.
post.summary: Post a comment on a Ramp object (transaction, bill, reimbursement, etc)

post.description: If a user is trying to mark their transaction as no receipt, use the MarkTransactionMissingReceipt tool. If the user is responding to a message that is asking for a reason for the user's receipt, then call this tool.
post.summary: Only call this tool if we directly ask for a no receipt reason
